author: Claw-Eval authors

readme.entry: - **[Claw-Eval: Towards Trustworthy Evaluation of Autonomous Agents](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06132)** — Claw-Eval authors — <https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06132> · *paper/benchmark* — Autonomous-agent evaluation suite with completion, safety, robustness, and auditable evidence channels. 🆕

section: 9 · Agent-specific evaluation (trajectories, tool use, multi-turn, world state, multi-agent, localization)
source_seam: new eval benchmarks/tools via Exa + arXiv API
title: Claw-Eval: Towards Trustworthy Evaluation of Autonomous Agents

worker.candidate_reason: Clears the bar because it explicitly tackles trajectory-opaque grading with 300 human-verified tasks, execution traces, service-side audit logs, environment snapshots, fine-grained rubrics, and pass@k/pass^k scoring.
worker.evidence: arXiv abstract fetched in evidence-arxiv-selected.xml describes 300 human-verified tasks across 9 categories, three evidence channels (execution traces, audit logs, environment snapshots), 2,159 rubric items, and Completion/Safety/Robustness scoring with Average Score, Pass@k, and Pass^k.
worker.summary: Autonomous-agent evaluation suite with completion, safety, robustness, and auditable evidence channels.
